issues: blue-cool color temperature conflicts with warm/sunny target palette
peer reference helmets skew dark/desaturated dungeon-crawler rather than bright fairy-tale
teleport disc effect reads clinical/sterile rather than whimsical toy-diorama
insufficient visual warmth — lacks the golden/amber/coral hues expected for Hyrule-sunny aesthetic
reasoning: Candidate (RIGHT): circular scroll/teleport disc rendered in white and blue-teal tones with a small character figure inside. The effect is clean and light, avoiding grimdark or edgy territory, but falls into 'cold-corporate/clinical' risk zone due to its cool blue-white palette. The sparkle/glow elements give a hint of magic but feel more sci-fi portal than fairy-tale whimsy. Peer references (LEFT): a grid of pixel-art helmets in muted gray, black, and minimal gold — these themselves lean toward desaturated dungeon-crawler aesthetic rather than bright warm Nintendo-family tone, suggesting broader project aesthetic drift. The candidate does not introduce any hard FAIL elements (no grimness, no fan-service, no occult symbols), but the cool palette and sterile circular form do not hit the 'bright/warm/sunny Hyrule / toy-diorama / wholesome' target. Needs warmer hues (amber, gold, soft pink/lavender) and more playful/organic shape language to fully pass.
